en.extract:
The Bornean crestless fireback (Lophura pyronota) is a member of the Phasianidae. It was previously known as the crestless fireback when it was lumped together with the species malayan crestless fireback. The two species were separated into two monotypic species based on obvious plumage differences, most notably the dark plumage of the male, which shows silvery white streaks.
The Bornean crestless fireback is found in northern Borneo.

[[File:Bornean Crestless Fireback by George Edward Lodge.png|thumb|Illustration by [[George Edward Lodge]]]]
The '''Bornean crestless fireback''' ('''''Lophura pyronota''''') is a member of the Phasianidae. It was previously known as the crestless fireback when it was lumped together with the species [[malayan crestless fireback]]. The two species were separated into two [[monotypic species]] based on obvious plumage differences,<ref>{{Cite web |title=The Checklist v2025 |url=http://www.avilist.org/checklist/v2025/ |access-date=2026-01-25 |website=[[AviList]]: The Global Avian Checklist |language=en-US |doi=10.2173/avilist.v2025}}</ref> most notably the dark plumage of the male, which shows silvery white streaks.<ref>{{Cite journal|title=Malayan Crestless Fireback (Lophura erythrophthalma), version 1.0|url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/species/crefir5/cur/introduction|journal=Birds of the World|date=2022|issn=2771-3105|doi=10.2173/bow.crefir5.01|language=en|first=Josep|last=del Hoyo|first2=Philip J. K.|last2=McGowan|first3=Guy M.|last3=Kirwan|first4=Nigel|last4=Collar|first5=David|last5=Christie|url-access=subscription}}</ref>

The Bornean crestless fireback is found in northern [[Borneo]].
